title: Learning shapes cortical dynamics to enhance integration of relevant sensory input

frameworks.name: recurrent neural network dynamics
frameworks.one_line: cortex modeled as recurrently connected circuit with integrating modes shaping coding

frameworks.name: excitatory-inhibitory circuit model
frameworks.one_line: E/I connectivity changes among tuned neurons realign integrating modes

frameworks.name: perceptual learning
frameworks.one_line: discrimination training drives task-relevant cortical reorganization

core_question: How does learning reshape recurrent cortical dynamics to better integrate task-relevant sensory input?
key_assumptions: sensory coding depends on recurrent circuit dynamics, not just feedforward drive
information in network output depends on alignment between input and integrating modes
E/I connectivity between tuned neurons determines slow integrating modes
manipulations: training mice on visual feature discrimination over learning
comparison of feedforward input alignment with circuit integrating modes
varying excitatory-inhibitory connectivity among feature-tuned units in model
key_findings: information in network output varies with alignment between input and integrating modes
slow patterns of cortical dynamics realigned over learning to integrate task-relevant input
realignment explained by E/I connectivity changes among neurons tuned to relevant features
interpretations: learning tunes temporal dynamics of cortical circuits to optimally integrate relevant input
recurrent circuit reorganization, not just tuning changes, supports adaptive sensory behavior
